overview: This image appears to show a Central American bushmaster (Lachesis stenophrys), a large, venomous pit viper native to Central American forests. The snake is characterized by a prominent middorsal ridge, which is most distinct on the last quarter of its body. It is considered the longest venomous snake in the Western Hemisphere, with adults typically measuring between 190 to 210 centimeters. The species name Lachesis muta means "silent fate," referring to its habit of shaking its tail, though it lacks a rattle. iNaturalist +3 4 sites Central American Bushmaster (Lachesis stenophrys) · iNaturalist
